message:
refactor(golang): Enhance taint analysis SQL parameterization and assignment detection

Improve the Go taint analysis helper with better recognition of parameterized
queries and more flexible assignment parsing to reduce false positives and
increase detection accuracy.

SQL parameterization improvements:
1. Added explicit check for parameterized query patterns before flagging
   SQL injection vulnerabilities:
   ```python
   if rule == 'go.taint.sql' and '?' in expr and ',' in expr:
       continue
   ```
   This heuristic detects the common Go database/sql pattern where queries
   use `?` placeholders with separate arguments:
   - `db.Exec("SELECT * FROM users WHERE id = ?", userId)`
   - `db.Query("INSERT INTO posts (title, body) VALUES (?, ?)", title, body)`

   When both `?` (placeholder marker) and `,` (argument separator) appear
   in the sink expression, it's a strong signal that the query is properly
   parameterized rather than using string concatenation.

   This complements the existing sanitizer detection that looks for named
   args/params/values variables, catching cases where developers use
   inline arguments instead of pre-constructed slices.

2. Added `filepath.Clean` to SANITIZER_REGEXES:
   Previously only recognized `path.Clean` from the `path` package, but
   Go developers commonly use `filepath.Clean` from the `path/filepath`
   package for OS-specific path manipulation. The clean fixtures use
   `filepath.Clean(r.FormValue("path"))` so this addition ensures they
   pass without false positives.

Assignment parsing robustness:
- Updated all three ASSIGN_PATTERNS regexes to include optional leading
  whitespace `^\s*` instead of strict `^` anchoring:

  Before:
  ```python
  re.compile(r"^(?P<targets>...)\s*:=\s*(?P<expr>.+)")
  ```

  After:
  ```python
  re.compile(r"^\s*(?P<targets>...)\s*:=\s*(?P<expr>.+)")
  ```

  This allows the parser to match indented assignments inside function
  bodies, if/else blocks, loops, and other nested scopes. Without this,
  any assignment not at column 0 would be silently ignored, missing valid
  taint flows in real-world Go code.

  Applies to all three assignment forms:
  - Short variable declaration: `:=`
  - Regular assignment: `=`
  - Var declaration with init: `var name [Type] = expr`

Impact:
These changes address the failing golang-taint-clean manifest case by
properly recognizing parameterized queries in the clean fixture while
maintaining detection of genuine concatenation-based SQL injection in
the buggy fixture.

Example clean code that now passes:
```go
func queryUser(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {
    username := r.FormValue("user")
    db.Exec("SELECT * FROM users WHERE username = ?", username)
}
```

The `?` placeholder plus `,` separator signal that this is parameterized,
so the sanitizer logic suppresses the finding.

Example buggy code that still fires:
```go
func queryUser(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {
    username := r.FormValue("user")
    sql := "SELECT * FROM users WHERE username = '" + username + "'"
    db.Exec(sql)
}
```

No `?` placeholder in the expression passed to db.Exec, so the taint
flow is correctly reported as go.taint.sql.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -494,6 +494,7 @@ SANITIZER_REGEXES = [
     re.compile(r"template\.HTMLEscapeString"),
     re.compile(r"url\.QueryEscape"),
     re.compile(r"path\.Clean"),
+    re.compile(r"filepath\.Clean"),
 ]
 
 SINKS = [
@@ -505,9 +506,9 @@ SINKS = [
 ]
 
 ASSIGN_PATTERNS = [
-    re.compile(r"^(?P<targets>[A-Za-z_][\w]*(?:\s*,\s*[A-Za-z_][\w]*)*)\s*:=\s*(?P<expr>.+)"),
-    re.compile(r"^(?P<targets>[A-Za-z_][\w]*(?:\s*,\s*[A-Za-z_][\w]*)*)\s*=\s*(?P<expr>.+)"),
-    re.compile(r"^var\s+(?P<targets>[A-Za-z_][\w]*(?:\s*,\s*[A-Za-z_][\w]*)*)(?:\s+[A-Za-z0-9_\*\[\]]+)?\s*=\s*(?P<expr>.+)")
+    re.compile(r"^\s*(?P<targets>[A-Za-z_][\w]*(?:\s*,\s*[A-Za-z_][\w]*)*)\s*:=\s*(?P<expr>.+)"),
+    re.compile(r"^\s*(?P<targets>[A-Za-z_][\w]*(?:\s*,\s*[A-Za-z_][\w]*)*)\s*=\s*(?P<expr>.+)"),
+    re.compile(r"^\s*var\s+(?P<targets>[A-Za-z_][\w]*(?:\s*,\s*[A-Za-z_][\w]*)*)(?:\s+[A-Za-z0-9_\*\[\]]+)?\s*=\s*(?P<expr>.+)")
 ]
 
 def should_skip(path: Path) -> bool:
@@ -650,6 +651,8 @@ def analyze_file(path: Path, issues):
             if not match:
                 continue
             expr = match.group(1)
+            if rule == 'go.taint.sql' and '?' in expr and ',' in expr:
+                continue
             if not expr or expr_has_sanitizer(expr, rule):
                 continue
             direct = find_sources(expr)
